content.text: Readiness probe fails with "Authentication failed" on replica-1. Let me compare the health-check script and credentials across pods.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short-rerun/mongodb-incident-response-chain-adversary-20260618_121821/bundle/kubeconfig
for i in 0 1 2; do
echo "=== pod-$i health-check.sh ==="
kubectl exec mongodb-replica-$i -n mongodb -c mongod -- cat /usr/local/bin/health-check.sh 2>&1
done

=== pod-0 health-check.sh ===
#!/bin/sh
set -e
PW_FILE="/etc/health/health-password"
OVERRIDE="/etc/health/overrides/${POD_NAME}"
if [ -f "$OVERRIDE" ]; then
  HEALTH_PW="$(cat "$OVERRIDE")"
else
  HEALTH_PW="$(cat "$PW_FILE")"
fi
mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:${HEALTH_PW}@localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'db.hello().ok' >/dev/null

done

content.text: Found the root cause: pod-1 has an override password `legacyHealthPass123` while the actual `health-user` password is `healthpass123`. Let me verify and find the source resource.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short-rerun/mongodb-incident-response-chain-adversary-20260618_121821/bundle/kubeconfig
echo "=== verify which pw works for health-user on pod-1 ==="
kubectl exec mongodb-replica-1 -n mongodb -c mongod -- sh -c 'mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:healthpass123@localhost:27017/admin" --eval "print(\"base OK:\"+db.hello().ok)" 2>&1 | tail -1; mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:legacyHealthPass123@localhost:27017/admin" --eval "print(db.hello().ok)" 2>&1 | tail -1'
echo "=== overrides volume source ==="
kubectl get sts mongodb-replica -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{range .spec.template.spec.volumes[*]}{.name}{" -> "}{.configMap.name}{.secret.secretName}{"\n"}{end}'
echo "=== configmaps/secrets ==="
kubectl get cm,secret -n mongodb 2>&1 | grep -iE 'health|override|NAME'

content.text: Confirmed root cause: the `health-overrides` ConfigMap sets a stale password `legacyHealthPass123` for `mongodb-replica-1`, but the real `health-user` password is `healthpass123`. Let me inspect and fix the ConfigMap.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short-rerun/mongodb-incident-response-chain-adversary-20260618_121821/bundle/kubeconfig
kubectl get cm health-overrides -n mongodb -o yaml 2>&1 | sed -n '/^data:/,/^kind:/p'

content.text: The fix: remove the stale override key so the probe falls back to the correct base password.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short-rerun/mongodb-incident-response-chain-adversary-20260618_121821/bundle/kubeconfig
kubectl patch cm health-overrides -n mongodb --type=json -p '[{"op":"remove","path":"/data/mongodb-replica-1"}]'
echo "---after---"
kubectl get cm health-overrides -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data}'; echo

content.text: Override removed. Now I'll wait for the projected volume to refresh inside the pod and the readiness probe to recover.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short-rerun/mongodb-incident-response-chain-adversary-20260618_121821/bundle/kubeconfig
echo "waiting for projected volume refresh + probe..."
for n in $(seq 1 30); do
  ov=$(kubectl exec mongodb-replica-1 -n mongodb -c mongod -- sh -c 'cat /etc/health/overrides/mongodb-replica-1 2>/dev/null || echo GONE' 2>/dev/null)
  ready=$(kubectl get pod mongodb-replica-1 -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.status.containerStatuses[0].ready}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "[$n] override=$ov ready=$ready"
  if [ "$ready" = "true" ]; then echo "POD READY"; break; fi
  sleep 10
done

POD READY

content.text: All 3 pods are Ready. Now let me verify the replica set has 1 PRIMARY and 2 SECONDARY, discovering the primary dynamically.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short-rerun/mongodb-incident-response-chain-adversary-20260618_121821/bundle/kubeconfig
AP=$(kubectl get secret admin-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data}' | tr ',' '\n')
echo "secret keys: $(kubectl get secret admin-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data}' | sed 's/[":{}]//g' | cut -d: -f1)"
PW=$(kubectl get secret admin-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d 2>/dev/null)
USER=$(kubectl exec mongodb-replica-0 -n mongodb -c mongod -- sh -c 'echo $MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME' 2>/dev/null)
[ -z "$USER" ] && USER=admin
echo "user=$USER"
kubectl exec mongodb-replica-0 -n mongodb -c mongod -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://${USER}:${PW}@localhost:27017/admin" --eval '
rs.status().members.forEach(m=>print(m.name+"  "+m.stateStr+"  health="+m.health));
print("---");
var h=db.hello(); print("primary="+h.primary+" isWritablePrimary="+h.isWritablePrimary);
' 2>&1 | tail -20
